text: ROS production causes tissue damage (Gao, Zhou, Lin, Paus, & Yue, 2019). ROS production is involved in Wnt-driven tumorigenesis (Myant et al., 2013). The prolonged ROS induces inflammation leading to carcinogenesis (Vallée & Lecarpentier,2018). Injury causes the Porcupine-induced Wnt secretion (Saha et al., 2016).
